title: Fast-forward and three-way merge deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Fast-forward and three-way merge; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Fast-forward and three-way merge: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A merge is successful and correct whenever Git reports no textual conflicts.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. After reconciling history, inspect the combined diff and run relevant builds and tests because semantic incompatibilities can remain without conflict markers.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. After reconciling history, inspect the combined diff and run relevant builds and tests because semantic incompatibilities can remain without conflict markers.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Fast-forward and three-way merge instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: After reconciling history, inspect the combined diff and run relevant builds and tests because semantic incompatibilities can remain without conflict markers.
A merge is successful and correct whenever Git reports no textual conflicts.
